page: Amazon Web Services c7i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance powered by custom Intel Xeon 8488C processors built on the x86_64 architecture. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs derived from 2 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled. The system is equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory operating at 5 GHz, yielding a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. It runs on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, provides a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps, and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Performance benchmarks indicate average single-core capabilities, with stress-ng and Geekbench single-core tests placing in the middle 50% tier. However, multi-core performance and memory bandwidth fall into the bottom 25% tier, reflecting the resource limits of a 4-vCPU configuration. Notably, the processor supports advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512, which assist in specific vector and matrix operations. Geekbench text processing is a relative strength, scoring in the top 25% tier, while static web serving and small-model LLM inference perform at average levels.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the c7i.xlarge offers a dedicated compute profile with modern DDR5 memory and advanced instruction support without the overhead of bundled local storage. It is best suited for compute-bound workloads that benefit from single-core efficiency and vector or matrix instructions, such as lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic utility scripting, rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized multi-core applications.
description: Amazon Web Services c7i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture, it includes 8.0 GB of DDR5 memory at 5 GHz, but lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Built on the Nitro hypervisor, the instance delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 2 Gbps. Benchmark data shows average single-core CPU performance and strong text processing capabilities, though multi-core execution and memory bandwidth reside in lower percentiles. The processor supports advanced vector and matrix extensions (AVX-512 and AMX), making it qualitatively cost-efficient for specialized compute tasks. This instance is best suited for lightweight web serving, text processing, and single-threaded utility workloads.
og_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance featuring 4 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB of DDR5 RAM, and advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and AMX.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services c7i.xlarge is a compute-optimized instance with 4 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 8.0 GB DDR5 RAM, and a 2 Gbps network baseline.
tagline: A compute-optimized virtual server featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, DDR5 memory, and advanced vector extensions for specialized workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 8488C processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 2 physical cores
Equipped with 8.0 GB of high-speed DDR5 memory at 5 GHz
Supports advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512
Delivers strong text processing and average single-core benchmark performance
Configured with a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth and no local storage
Optimized for lightweight web serving, text processing, and basic compute tasks
